description:
Parent epic: V3 E5: Controls and human in the loop

Source task 6/9 from MILESTONE_V3.md:

- Define how controls interact with typed results, retries, schedules,
  subagents, workflows, and handoffs.

Epic closure criteria:
- Controls are the one public policy concept
- human-in-the-loop is demonstrated without new runtime machinery
- raising controls cannot crash the agent process

comments.text: Documented the control interaction contract: input controls run before memory/compaction/provider calls, operation controls guard actions/workflows/subagents/handoffs, result controls run after typed result parsing, schedules use the normal chat path, and blocked/interrupted controls are not retried.

title: E5: Define how controls interact with typed results, retries, schedules, subagents,...
